content.raw_text: Neon Edge | Module 15: The Solvency Breach The Solvency Breach Chapter 15: Capital Structure & Creditors' Rights UVTA PROTOCOL: ACTIVE ⚖ Creditor HUD Insolvency (UVTA § 2) A debtor is insolvent if the sum of its debts is greater than the sum of its assets at a **fair valuation**. Voidable Transfer A transfer is voidable if made: (1) with **actual intent** to hinder creditors, or (2) without receiving **reasonably equivalent value** while insolvent. Balance Alert Creditors have frozen the primary accounts. Sammy is looking for "Backdoors" to move the architectural haptic-shards before the liquidation. Solvency Balance Sheet Total Assets (Credits + Tech) 40,000,000 Total Liabilities (Judgments) 50,000,000 Status: INSOLVENT (Balance Sheet Test) I. The Diversion Console Sammy has identified three ways to "save" the assets. Analyze each move. If the transfer is Voidable under the UVTA, it will be flagged by the Council's auditors. Insider Payout Transfer 5M credits to Zeeva's personal vault as "Retrospective Bonus." The Gift Shell Transfer Haptic-Shard ownership to "Alpha-Holdings" (owned by Sammy) for 1 Credit. Ordinary Debt Paydown Pay 10M to the Steel Supplier for last month's inventory. Audit Findings: Badges of Fraud Creditor Litigation: Final Record "The attempt to move credits to Zeeva and Sammy while SP Corp was ... Solvent Insolvent triggers the protections of the UVTA. Because the transfers involved ... Insiders (Founders) Strangers and lacked ... Legal Advice Reasonably Equivalent Value , they are deemed ... Valid Payments Voidable Fraudulent Transfers that the court will claw back for the benefit of the creditors." Submit Court Filing Acknowledge
